title: 10 best herbal teas for coughing fits
intro_desc: Herbal teas for coughing fits are a natural remedy that offers relief from persistent and bothersome coughs. These teas work by soothing the throat, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus, providing instant comfort to those suffering from coughing fits. Examples of herbal teas that help with coughing fits include peppermint, ginger, and licorice root tea, which have anti-inflammatory properties that ease congestion and calm the respiratory system. Drinking these teas regularly can significantly improve quality of life by reducing cough frequency and intensity, allowing individuals to sleep better, work more efficiently, and enjoy daily activities without interruption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with coughing fits because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that provide natural relief from respiratory issues. The tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause persistent coughs, while its anti-inflammatory agents soothe irritated airways and reduce mucus production. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties loosen and clear out phlegm, allowing for easier breathing and a more productive cough, ultimately providing relief from those pesky coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like terpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with coughing fits.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: The tea's antiviral and antimicrobial properties may help combat the underlying viral or bacterial infection causing the cough, reducing its severity and duration.

Expectorant and demulcent properties: Purple coneflower tea is rich in expectorants like mucilage, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel during a coughing fit.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated throats, making them a popular choice for tea blends to alleviate coughing fits.
Barks: The bark of the purple coneflower contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it an effective ingredient in teas for relieving coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Boil one cup of filtered water and pour it over the echinacea flowers, allowing for a steeping time of 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug, discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste, as desired, and stir well to combine with the cooled tea, taking about 2-3 minutes.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation, ideally within 15-30 minutes of brewing to maximize effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with coughing fits because of its rich antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work together to soothe the throat and reduce irritation caused by coughs. Additionally, maidenhair tree teas contain expectorants that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel the cough-causing irritant. As a result, regular consumption of maidenhair tree teas can provide natural relief from persistent and troublesome coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antitussive compounds: The tea contains antitussive compounds like ginkgolides and bilobalide, which help to suppress the cough reflex and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Inflammation-reducing properties: Maidenhair Tree tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the respiratory tract, reducing swelling and irritation that may be contributing to coughing fits.
Expectorant action: The tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks have astringent properties that help reduce mucus production and ease coughs.
Seeds: With their high concentration of fatty acids, seeds are used to relieve bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and relaxing airways.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and pour into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Bring fresh water to a boil in a kettle or on the stovetop and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled water over the ginkgo biloba leaves and let steep for 10-15 minutes to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Serve the tea hot or warm, adding honey or lemon to taste if desired, and enjoy immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to soothe irritated respiratory passages, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel, providing relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antibacterial properties that help combat infection-causing bacteria in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating coughs.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in thyme, such as camphor and borneol, have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to ease congestion and soothe a sore throat, which can contribute to coughing fits.

Antioxidants: Thyme tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the severity of coughing fits caused by environmental pollutants or other irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in volatile oils, which help to soothe and calm coughs, making them an effective ingredient in herbal teas.
Leaves: Fresh or dried thyme leaves contain a compound called thymol, which has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve congestion and coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves or use dried thyme flowers according to recommended dosages.
Measure two teaspoons of dried thyme flowers or one tablespoon of fresh thyme leaves into a teapot.
Boil one cup of pure water for exactly five minutes and carefully pour it over the herbs in the teapot.
Allow the herbal mixture to steep for ten to fifteen minutes to release optimal essential oils and flavors.
Strain the tea and drink it hot immediately, ideally before bedtime or as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughs. The herbal tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for coughing fits. Overall, English lavender teas provide a natural and effective way to alleviate the discomfort and distress caused by coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender tea's antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can ease congestion and soothe a cough.

Expectorant Properties: The expectorant properties of lavender tea help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender tea's calming and sedative effects can help relax the throat and respiratory muscles, reducing spasms and discomfort that can trigger or exacerbate coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and calm coughs.
Flowers: The flowers of the English lavender plant contain esters, which have expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and one cup of boiling water for the infusion process.
Steep the dried lavender flowers in the boiling water for eight to ten minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot to remove any solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before adding one tablespoon of honey and one lemon slice as desired.
Enjoy the soothing and calming effects of the lavandula angustifolia tea within 30 minutes to an hour after preparation for optimal relief from coughing fits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural soothing properties. The herbs in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and volatile oils, work together to calm the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus. As a result, the constant hacking and coughing associated with respiratory issues subsides, allowing you to breathe more easily and get back to your day. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects can also help ease stress and anxiety that may exacerbate coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea helps relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing spasms and coughing fits by calming down irritated tissues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in lemon balm tea have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughs and congestion.
Expectorant properties: Lemon balm tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving chest congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a popular choice for making teas as they contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract during coughing fits.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are used to make teas that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, providing relief from persistent coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves in a clean container for future use.
Measure one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves per eight ounce cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbs in boiling water for five to seven minutes to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove solids and sediment.
Drink the cooled melissa officinalis tea within 24 hours of preparation or store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural antitussive properties, which help to soothe and calm the throat and lungs. The tea's active compounds, including artemesia and apigenin, work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate spasms in the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughing. Additionally, joe pye weed tea's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and clear out congestion. As a result, herbal joe pye weed teas offer a natural and effective remedy for alleviating coughs and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Joe Pye Weed tea can reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat and lungs, which can alleviate coughing fits and make breathing easier.

Antiviral properties: Joe Pye Weed's antiviral properties can help to combat viral infections that cause coughing, such as bronchitis or pneumonia, by inhibiting the growth of viruses and reducing their ability to multiply.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and tannins, which help soothe irritated throats and reduce inflammation caused by coughs.
Rhyzomes: Containing expectorant properties, they help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of fresh material.
Measure one cup of boiling water and pour it over the herb in a heat-resistant glass teapot or mug.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes allowing the herbs to infuse their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into another container discarding the solids and serving hot or at room temperature.
Consume one to two cups of the eupatorium perfoliatum tea as needed up to three times a day for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and calm irritated tissues. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, providing quick relief from persistent coughs and colds. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help relax the body and quiet a nagging cough, allowing for a peaceful night's sleep and faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can ease congestion and soothe a sore throat.

Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile tea have been shown to relax the smooth muscles in the bronchi and lungs, helping to relieve coughing spasms and reduce mucus production.

Sedative and expectorant properties: Chamomile's sedative effects can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. Its expectorant properties also help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in teas for coughing fits.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the throat and lungs, reducing the severity of coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a small bowl.
Heat one cup of water in a saucepan over medium heat for five minutes.
Add the measured matricaria chamomilla flowers to the boiling water and steep for seven to nine minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Allow the tea to cool for two to three minutes before drinking slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with coughing fits because it is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce irritation and swelling in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs. Additionally, dandelion root has been shown to have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing fast relief from hacking coughs and congestion.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Dandelion tea contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated throats and airways, providing relief from persistent coughing.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea help reduce inflammation and combat oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to chronic coughs and respiratory issues.

Inulin: Dandelion root contains inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health and immune function, which can help alleviate symptoms of persistent coughing and bronchitis by promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich source of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm the throat and lungs during coughing fits.
Buds: The buds are utilized because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and relieve congestion associated with coughing.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Allow the measured leaves to steep in the hot water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add one tablespoon of raw honey to sweeten the tea if desired and stir well.
Drink the tea immediately while it is still warm, ideally within thirty minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe the throat and airways, reducing irritation and discomfort that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, lemon verbena's antibacterial properties may help combat underlying infections that contribute to persistent coughing. By promoting healthy respiratory function and alleviating congestion, herbal lemon verbena teas offer a soothing and effective remedy for managing coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Lemon verbena's astringent properties help to reduce mucus production and inflammation in the throat, providing relief from coughs and congestion.
Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the frequency of coughing fits.

Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's antispasmodic properties help to relax the smooth muscles in the throat and bronchial tubes, reducing spasms and relieving coughing fits by calming down the irritated tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe irritated throats and reduce coughing.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenes that provide a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried verbena officinalis leaves or 3-4 grams of fresh leaves into a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of pure water in a kettle and pour it over the verbena leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes depending on desired tea strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose herbs.
Drink the tea hot, with honey or lemon if desired, and repeat as needed up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with coughing fits because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The essential oils in wild marjoram, such as thymol and borneol, help to soothe the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and loosening mucus. This allows for easier removal of excess phlegm, providing relief from persistent coughing fits. Additionally, wild marjoram's warming properties can help to ease congestion, making it an effective natural remedy for calming coughs and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Wild marjoram's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and making it easier to cough up phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways and throat, which can help alleviate coughing fits caused by respiratory infections or allergies.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Wild marjoram's antiviral and antibacterial properties may help combat viral and bacterial infections that cause coughing fits, such as the common cold or bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant properties, the roots help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from congestion and coughs.
Seeds: With their warm and soothing properties, the seeds can help calm a cough and reduce inflammation in the throat and chest.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried or fresh origanum vulgare leaves and flowers from the garden or store-bought quantities are acceptable.
Measure two teaspoons of dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers per cup of boiling water for optimal potency.
Bring one cup of pure filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized pot and remove from heat immediately.
Steep the measured origanum vulgare leaves and flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release essential oils.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids before serving.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat coughing fits with herbal teas is to brew a soothing cup of peppermint or thyme tea, which helps calm the throat and reduce inflammation. Ginger tea adds warmth and comfort, while eucalyptus tea's decongestant properties help loosen phlegm. Try combining these herbs in one tea blend for maximum relief. Drink hot, as it helps ease coughing spasms, or chilled to soothe a sore throat.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to coughing fits that are treated with herbal teas are bronchitis, asthma, and sinus infections. Herbs like ginger, peppermint, and eucalyptus help to loosen mucus, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. These herbs can be consumed as hot or cold tea infusions to provide rapid relief from symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and chest tightness. Additionally, herbal teas containing licorice root and slippery elm may also soothe irritated throats and calm the respiratory system.
